label: Narrow-shell 1BR / 1 bath, FRONT-REAR — the bedroom owns the full-width rear band; in front of it, a full-depth great room column (hosts the entry) runs beside a wet column with the kitchen above the bath on one plumbing wall (bath at the front corner, door off the great room). Dining at a counter on the kitchen/great seam. Based on the narrow-fp-02 reference plan (3.2 x 7.0 m micro-unit).

adjacencies.note: bedroom's only door — opens south from the full-width rear band into the great room column. 1.2 m floor = 0.9 m clear door + frames.
the kitchen's rear edge meets the bedroom band's underside on the left column — geometry-only shared wall, no door. Keeps the seam between the front zone and the rear band continuous.
bath (front) and kitchen (above it) stack in the left column on one plumbing wall — shared wall only, no door.
bath's only door — opens east into the great room near the entry, matching the reference plan. No bath-into-kitchen sanitary trade-off in this topology.

kitchen and great room sit SIDE-BY-SIDE (vertical seam) — open plan with a dining counter along the shared edge, band + 2 stools inside the great room (counter_side) so the kitchen keeps its full work aisle; mirrors the reference plan's counter-attached table. min_shared_wall_m 2.1 = 2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass. Requires ldk_horizontal.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_nw_front_rear_bath_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Compact super-deep unit (canonical 4x8 m envelope, 32 sqm — reference plan is 22.4). Same tier values as the other 1BR topologies: bath floors at 2.6 sqm and caps at 4.5 so snap_gaps can't balloon it; bedroom caps at 9.5 — with the full 4 m width anchored to both side walls, that pins the rear band's depth at ~2.4 m and pushes the freed depth into the great room.

notes: Third 1BR entry in the narrow catalog, and the first use of the locked
'front_rear' strategy token: the bedroom owns the FULL-WIDTH rear band,
and everything else sits in front of it. Modeled on the narrow-fp-02
reference plan (a 3.2 x 7.0 m / 22.4 sqm Brazilian micro-unit): bedroom
across the rear with a service/laundry area behind it, living in the
middle with a counter-attached table, kitchen run along one side wall,
bath at the front corner, entry on the front wall beside the bath.
Layout intent (front at bottom, canonical 4 x 8 m envelope):
Rear:   [BEDROOM  4.0 x ~2.4  (full width)      ]
Middle: [KITCHEN 1.5x~3.6 | GREAT ROOM (cont.)  ]
Front:  [BATH    1.5x~2.0 | GREAT ~2.5 full-depth]
[porch/entry]
Canonical fit: 8 x 12 m lot with standard 2 m setbacks -> 4 x 8 m envelope
(ratio 0.5, 'super_deep' category, 32 sqm floor). Roomier than the 24-25
sqm tiny-house set — the ~14 sqm great room clears the couch+TV+dining
furnishability band, though dining stays at the counter per the reference.
Global-rule overrides (all topology-scoped):
- kitchen_rear_pin: false — the bedroom owns the rear wall; the kitchen
sits mid-column with the bath in front of it. Same flag as the
front_back_split sibling; briefs should set kitchen_back_door: false
(the kitchen has no exterior rear wall).
- ldk_horizontal: true — kitchen beside the great room (vertical seam),
and the kitchen sits in the LEFT half (symmetry break would flip it).
- private_area_floor: false — bedroom ~9.4 sqm vs great+kitchen ~19.6.
- aspect_overrides great 2.3 — the full-depth great column runs
~2.5 x 5.65 (2.26:1), past the strict 1.8 cap (its width stays under
the 3.0 m chunky-relax threshold).
No zone_split: the full-width bedroom crosses both columns, so no single
straight private/public line exists — the two front_to_rear_stacks
([bath, kitchen, bedroom] and [great, bedroom]) plus side anchors pin the
structure instead. The bedroom is anchored to BOTH side walls, which
forces its full-width span; with the 9.5 sqm cap that pins the rear
band's depth at ~2.4 m.
Wet stack: bath and kitchen share one vertical plumbing wall; the rear
service_area (laundry, per the reference's washer) sits in the rear
setback behind the bedroom. No dirty kitchen declared — the kitchen has
no rear exposure.
Bath door opens into the GREAT room (as in the reference) — no
bath-into-kitchen sanitary warning.
No fallback declared — the other narrow 1BR topologies have different
structural patterns (corridor shotgun, stepped front-back split) rather
than being relaxations of this one.
